seo.title: Did I Pass AP Psych? Estimate Your AP Psychology Score
seo.description: Wondering if you passed AP Psych? Estimate your AP Psychology score and see whether your MCQ and FRQ performance may be enough for a 3 or higher.

content.directAnswer: A score of 3 or higher is commonly treated as passing, but college credit policies vary. Use the calculator to estimate whether your performance may reach a 3.

sections.heading: What Counts as Passing AP Psych?
sections.body: Many students think of a 3 or higher as passing, but individual colleges decide whether a score earns credit.

sections.heading: Estimate Whether You Passed
sections.body: Enter your MCQ and FRQ estimates in the calculator to see whether your composite estimate lands in the 3+ range.

faqs.question: Is this calculator official?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator is an independent estimate and is not affiliated with or endorsed by College Board.

faqs.question: Can actual AP score cutoffs vary?
faqs.answer: Yes. Actual score thresholds can vary by year and exam form, so this tool should be used for planning only.

faqs.question: Does FRQ performance matter for AP Psych?
faqs.answer: Yes. FRQ performance contributes to the composite estimate, so a stronger FRQ score can offset some missed multiple-choice questions.

faqs.question: Should I use this before or after the exam?
faqs.answer: You can use it before the exam for target planning or after the exam to estimate possible score outcomes.
